item.title: Putin says Russia test launches "most powerful missile in the world"
item.description: Russia tested a new long-range missile capable of carrying nuclear warheads, months after the last treaty with the U.S. expired.

item.title: Sam Altman testifies in Elon Musk-OpenAI trial
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman took the stand Tuesday in Elon Musk's high-profile lawsuit against Altman's company. Altman defended the company against Musk's allegations that OpenAI betrayed its founding mission by becoming a for-profit entity. New York Times tech reporter Natallie Rocha joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Trump on "the only thing that matters" in dealing with Iran
item.description: President Trump said Americans' financial situation isn't motivating him to make a deal, "Not even a little bit," and that he is only focused on preventing Iran from getting nuclear weapons.

item.title: Feds charge ship operator in Baltimore's Key Bridge collapse
item.description: The operator of the Dali, a container ship that lost power and slammed into Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge in 2024, killing six people, is facing federal charges.

item.title: Businesses starting to receive their IEEPA tariff refunds
item.description: "I'd been checking the status feverishly to see if anything was in my bank account," one small business owner said.

item.title: Watch: Van Hollen and Patel spar over FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen and FBI director Kash Patel had a second tense exchange Tuesday during Patel's testimony before a Senate Appropriations subcommittee, this time over the firing of FBI agents.

item.title: Iran says it's ready to "teach a lesson" if U.S. launches new attacks
item.description: Iran warns any new U.S. attacks will bring a "bad result," as President Trump acknowledges the ceasefire is faltering and violence flares in Lebanon.

item.title: Searing U.S. energy prices are driving the hottest inflation in years
item.description: CPI gas price index has surged 28% from a year ago, while overall energy costs are up nearly 18%, new inflation data shows.

item.title: Brandon Clarke of the Memphis Grizzlies dies at age 29
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke has died at the age of 29, the team announced.

item.title: What to know about today's primaries in Nebraska and West Virginia
item.description: Voters are going to the polls in Nebraska and West Virginia on Tuesday, with Democrats vying for the chance to run in an open seat in Nebraska that the party has long been eyeing.

item.title: Watch: Patel clashes with Van Hollen about drinking allegations at Senate hearing
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen and FBI director Kash Patel had a heated moment Tuesday as Van Hollen questioned Patel about allegations of excessive drinking that were reported on by the Atlantic. The moment came during a Senate hearing on the 2027 fiscal budgets for various federal law enforcement agencies.

item.title: Marty Makary resigns as FDA commissioner after internal tensions
item.description: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary resigned his position, stepping aside amid a swirl of reports that his tenure was coming to an end.

item.title: Hegseth spars with Murphy after he questions narrative on Iran's nuclear weapon capacity
item.description: Connecticut Democratic Sen. Chris Murphy asked Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth about military options to open the Strait of Hormuz, and about reports of an apparent intelligence assessment on Iran's nuclear capacity that may contradict President Trump's past statements. The exchange occurred during a Senate panel hearing on the Pentagon's 2027 budget request.

item.title: Trump takes questions on Iran, inflation and China moments before departing for Beijing
item.description: Moments before President Trump left for his trip to Beijing on Tuesday, he took questions from reporters on the Iran war, inflation, and more. Following Mr. Trump's comments, CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi, Kelly O'Grady and Sam Vinograd provided context.

item.title: DOJ announces criminal charges against ship operator in Baltimore's Key Bridge collapse
item.description: The Justice Department announced criminal charges against the ship operator in the Francis Scott Key Bridge collapse that killed six people in 2024. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: California mayor faces 10 years in prison after pleading guilty to acting as covert Chinese agent
item.description: Eileen Wang, the former mayor of Arcadia, California, faces a 10-year prison sentence for pleading guilty to acting as a covert agent for China. CBS News Los Angeles' Tina Patel has more.

item.title: What we know about hantavirus cases tied to deadly cruise ship outbreak
item.description: Health officials have identified at least 11 confirmed or suspected cases of hantavirus tied to an outbreak on the M/V Hondius cruise ship.

item.title: Social Security's cost-of-living adjustment could reach nearly 4% in 2027
item.description: A larger COLA would boost monthly checks for retirees, but also strain Social Security's already depleted trust funds.

item.title: Americans from hantavirus-hit ship arrive in U.S., including 1 who tested positive
item.description: An American on the repatriation flight began showing symptoms of hantavirus and another "tested mildly PCR positive for the Andes virus," the Department of Health and Human Services says.

item.title: Republican redistricting efforts may not have intended impact, expert says
item.description: The Supreme Court cleared the way for Alabama to adopt a new congressional map for the 2026 midterm elections. CBS News contributor Kyle Kondik joins with more.

item.title: U.S. and Ukraine move closer to landmark drone defense deal, sources say
item.description: The U.S. and Ukraine have drafted an outline for a new defense deal that would allow Ukraine to export military technology to the U.S. and to manufacture drones with American companies, sources say. CBS News' Aidan Stretch has more.

item.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer rejects mounting calls to resign
item.description: Despite a fifth of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's fellow Labour Party lawmakers calling on him to step down, he says he'll "get on with governing."

item.title: Lawmakers press Hegseth on details on Iran war authorization, ceasefire
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the $1.5 trillion the Pentagon is seeking is "admittedly a historic budget."

item.title: Lawmakers push Hegseth for actual cost of Iran war during Capitol Hill hearing
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegesth and other Pentagon officials are testifying Tuesday on Capitol Hill over next year's proposed $1.5 trillion budget. CBS News' Nikole Killion has the latest.

item.title: Inflation accelerated in April to highest rate since May 2023
item.description: New inflation data shows a concerning trend in April as the Iran war impacts Americans' wallets. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady reports.

item.title: Ukraine and U.S. move toward landmark drone defense deal
item.description: A deal is taking shape for the U.S. and Ukraine to jointly develop and build weapons that have been at the forefront of the wars in both Ukraine and Iran.

item.title: EBay rejects GameStop's $55.5 billion takeover bid
item.description: GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen had argued that his company's retail locations would help eBay build a "national network."

item.title: Trump approves plan to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Marty Makary has served as Food and Drug Administration commissioner since March 2025.

item.title: Jimmy Lai's son says his father will die in prison if he's not saved soon
item.description: President Trump is set to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping this week in Beijing, where Taiwan is expected to be a key issue. Sebastien Lai, the son of imprisoned pro-democracy media mogul Jimmy Lai, told "CBS News Mornings" that his father will die in prison if he is not saved soon.

item.title: Bobbi Brown on her baby formula collaboration
item.description: Legendary makeup artist Bobbi Brown tells "CBS Saturday Morning" about her latest baby formula collaboration with Laura Modi's company Bobbie.
